observations.managementAction: To follow-up on request of additional staff under the Municipal Engineering Office

observations.recommendation:
Recommendations:
We recommended that Management implement the following remedial measures in compliance with IPSAS No. 17 and COA Circular No. 92-386:
Instruct the personnel in-charge to exhaust all possible means to facilitate the legal processing of ownership of the lots through a Transfer Certificates of Title (TCT) in the name of the Barangay, and seek budgetary allocations for the pertinent costs thereof; and
Provide copies of TCTs to the Municipal Accountant as basis for the establishment of a subsidiary list.

Recommendations:
We recommended that Management direct the following actions:
The Municipal Accounting Office, Municipal General Office, and Municipal Assessor’s Office should conduct a physical inventory of land properties following COA Circular 2020-006 and account/reconcile for the differences and variances between physical inventory and their respective records; and
The Inventory Committee should coordinate with the Municipal Assessor's Office to compare and reconcile land records herein after the physical count to ensure that all properties owned by the municipality are properly documented, valued, and reported in the financial statements. Any discrepancies should be promptly investigated and resolved.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Planning and Development Officer and the Municipal Engineer to:
a. 	Monitor and expedite the implementation of the remaining projects under the 20% LDF to ensure the timely delivery of essential services and maximize the socio-economic benefits for its constituents.
b. 	Convene the Local Development Council to revisit the LGU’s Annual Investment Plan and the 20% Local Development Fund Plan to determine and prioritize the implementation of feasible socio-economic programs, projects, and activities that will benefit the general welfare of its constituents following the provisions of Section 4 of DBM-DOF-DILG Circular No. 2020-01 and RA No. 7160.

Recommendations:
We reiterated our recommendation that Management instruct the concerned official from the BAC and/or the specific focal person tasked for this purpose to submit copies of all perfected contacts and POs, together with the complete supporting documents, to this office within five days from its perfection by Section 3.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009. Additionally, we recommend that the management consider hiring additional personnel to enhance the efficiency of the workload of the BAC Secretariat and expedite the submission process by regulatory timelines.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare a complete set of financial statements for each of the LGU’s economic enterprises. These statements shall be attached to the consolidated FS of the General Fund to provide immediate and adequate information on the enterprises' financial position, performance, and net assets/equity.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive:
a. 	Instruct the Municipal Accounting Office to immediately submit the lacking financial transaction documents and reports for December 2024 and regularly prepare and promptly submit monthly reports within the prescribed period.
b. 	Ensure that complete documentation is secured before payment so that only the act of filing at month-end can be undertaken, and
c. 	Conduct a study to determine the optimal number of employees under the Municipal Accounting Office to address volumetric duties assigned to the limited number of employees.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive and Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer (LDRRMO) stop charging administrative expenses to LDRRMF to provide sufficient funds for the LDRRM programs.

Recommendations:

We recommended that Management instruct the General Services Officer to furnish copies of Inspection and Acceptance Reports, together with the Delivery Receipts to the Audit Team within twenty-four hours from acceptance to enable them to conduct timely validation thereof pursuant to Section 6.9 of COA Circular No. 2009-002 dated May 18, 2009.

Recommendations:

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant to keep and maintain complete subsidiary records and financial statements, using the prescribed sub-codes for all the special accounts operated by the LGU and to submit copies of the same to the Management to provide them of the vital information to effectively manage and implement the different programs/projects and activities of these special accounts.
Such records and financial statements shall also be submitted and made available to this Office to facilitate our review/audit.
observations.reason: Special accounts have separate accounts. However still no subsidiary ledgers for special accounts.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendations:   
We recommended that Management direct:

1.	The Municipal Assessor to:

a.	Provide assessed values to the Municipal Treasurer for billing of the RPT based on the appraisal made by and received from the 
Provincial Assessor's Office; and

b.Ensure thereafter that all properties subject to RPT be included in the physical inventory to prevent under-assessment of taxes.  

2.	The Municipal Treasurer to prepare and submit billing of RPT to MORESCO I and to the Provincial Assessor’s Office / Treasurer.
observations.reason: No Tax Declaration of some electrical post

observations.recommendation:
Recommendations:

We recommended that Management require the BAC Secretariat to:
a. Fast track the sorting and collating of the subject contracts and supporting documents and immediately submit them to COA for evaluation and review, and thereafter ensure that copy of the purchase orders and perfected contracts be submitted to COA not later than five working days from issuance thereof;
b. If possible, make a formal request for the provision of full-time staff in the BAC Secretariat; and
c. For concerns on the staffing of the BAC Secretariat and all other concerns affecting the performance / output of the BAC, communicate such matters with concerned officials the soonest time possible for prompt action thereon.
observations.reason: Lack of Personnel

observations.recommendation:
Recommendations:
We recommended that management require the Municipal Engineer to hasten the conduct of re-inspection of projects and immediately submit to COA the pertinent requests for inspection and supporting documents for technical evaluation and inspection, and ensure thereafter the submission of said requests and documents within reasonable time after completion and acceptance of the infrastructure projects.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower BAC Secretariat is the same person who works with engineering

observations.recommendation:
Recommendations:
We recommended and Management is amenable to require the Municipal Accountant to reclassify the completed projects to their appropriate Property, Plant, and Equipment account.  We also recommended and for which Management also concurred to direct the Municipal Engineer to coordinate with the Municipal Accountant once constructed projects are completed and furnish the latter with the documents for proper reclassification to the appropriate accounts.

Recommendations:
We recommended and Management is agreeable to require the:
Municipal Treasurer to furnish the Municipal Accountant with a certified list of taxes due and collectible based on RPTAR/Taxpayer’s index card at the beginning of each year as basis in establishing the correct RPT and SET receivables; and
Municipal Accountant to maintain and update SLs of RPT and SET Receivables and reconcile the same with the records of the Municipal Treasurer to come up with accurate and reliable RPT and SET Receivable account balances

Recommendations:
We recommended and the Municipal Mayor agreed to instruct the:
HRMO to prioritize the updating of the salary adjustments or service records of the employees in the GSIS records.  Provide timeline to complete the updating of said records; and
Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to also prioritize the remittance of the amount of P3.516 million to the GSIS upon reconciliation/updating of the employee records.

Recommendations:
We recommended and Management agreed to the following:
a) Refrain from paying expenses out of the Petty cash Fund which are recurring in nature pursuant to Section 48 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume I; and
b) Procure the fuel/gasoline requirement of the Municipality from a supplier that extends credit line so that the monthly payment for the fuel/gasoline consumed can be paid directly to the supplier thru check. Stock withdrawal of gasoline should be accompanied by an approved vale slip indicating the type of vehicle to be used, purpose for which it will be used, quantity & type of fuel to be withdrawn and name of the driver.

Recommendation:
We recommended and management concurred to transfer the unexpended/unobligated balance of the LDRRMF and the Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund-MOOE to the trust fund account yearly.

Recommendations:
We reiterated our recommendation and for which Management agreed to instruct the concerned official from the BAC and/or the specific focal person tasked for this purpose, to submit copies of all perfected contacts and POs, together with the complete supporting documents, to this office within five days from its perfection in accordance with Section 3.1.1 of COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12, 2009. Additionally, we recommended and management agreed to consider hiring additional personnel to enhance the efficiency of the workload of the BAC Secretariat and expedite the submission process in accordance with regulatory timelines.

Recommendations:
We recommended and management affirmed the following:
Submit justifications/explanations for the said variances together with the corresponding supporting documents;
Direct the Municipal Engineering Office to strictly abide and enforce the rules and procedures in the preparation of cost estimates for all infrastructure projects and ensure that the basis and ceiling of bid prices are acceptable, reasonable, and advantageous to the government, thus, avoiding excessive contract cost; and
Assign or designate official/s to properly review and recompute to a reasonable accuracy the Program of Works (POW) and Detail Unit Price Analysis (DUPA) before approval and advertising.

Recommendations:
We recommended that management to direct the Municipal Accountant to record construction materials as “Construction Materials Inventory” and should only credit the same upon issuance thereof following the consolidated RIS and the SSMI, which should be prepared on a weekly basis. The aforementioned reports shall be the basis of the accounting department in the preparation of the JEV in the issuance of the construction materials.
We further recommended to the Management to observe sound inventory process reference to the NGAS, and also ensure the proper utilization of the storage room with dedicated Supply
Officer who should record and monitor the receipt and issuance of the inventories to facilitate reporting to the Accounting Office.

Recommendations:

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to keep and maintain the necessary subsidiary ledgers of all revenue collectors s to establish their accountability at any time and substantiate and reconciled with the GL balance.

Recommendations:
We recommended  Management to monitor and expedite the implementation of the remaining projects under the 20% LDF to adequately deliver the basic services to the constituents and maximize the socio-economic benefits intended to benefit its constituents. 

Convene the Local Development Council to consider revisiting the LGU’s Annual Investment Plan and the 20% Local Development Fund Plan to ascertain and prioritize the implementation of feasible socio-economic programs, projects and activities that will benefit the general welfare of its constituents in conformity with the provisions of Section 4 of DBM-DOF-DILG Circular No. 2020-01 and RA No. 7160.

Recommendations:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the concerned officials to prepare the LDRRMFIP with the details of the funded projects and activities for disaster mitigation, prevention and preparedness in the DRRM Investment Plan pursuant to 5.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We also recommended that Management require the concerned officials to prepare and submit a duly certified Report on Sources and Utilization of LDRRMF within the period prescribed, which is on or before the 15th day after the end of each month, in compliance with Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.
observations.reason: Report on the Sources and Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund (DRRMF) still not submitted within reglementary time.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendations:

We recommended that Management:

1. 	Direct the GSO- Designate or the MHO to make use of the RIS in recording the receipt of drugs/medicines and supplies and subsequent issuance thereof which will be used in the preparation and submission of the SSMI at the end of each week. 

2. 	Require the Accountant to:
 
 a. 	Strictly and consistently record procurement of drugs/medicines throughout the year following the perpetual inventory method using the Drugs and Medicines Inventory account and recognize expense for the subsequent issuances based on the SSMIs submitted by the GSO/MHO; 

 b. 	Identify and reclassify medical supplies recognized under the Drugs and Medicines Inventory account to the proper account Medical, Dental and Laboratory Supplies Inventory. 

3. In coordination with the Personnel Unit, review and evaluate the manning of the GSO and other units concerned, and consider detailing a staff to assist the existing personnel therein and to avoid multitasking.

Recommendations:

We recommended that management:

1. Instruct the concerned official/personnel to:

a. Hasten the preparation of documents necessary for the application for insurance of the properties; and
b. Revisit/refer to Section 5.1 of COA Circular No. 2018-002 for the preparation and submission of inventory reports of all insurable properties to GSIS to ensure that all insurable assets are adequately covered/insured with the GIF of the GSIS;
c. Ensure that a budget is provided for the insurance of all insurable properties and other assets under the custody and accountability of the Municipal government.
observations.reason:
Building Plans were still in the Province. 

Inventory for buildings including the computation of depreciation needs to be conducted

observations.recommendation:
Recommendations:

We recommended that Management:

a. Instruct the Municipal Accountant to appropriately recognize Leave Benefits Payable and Terminal Leave Benefits in the books;

b. Direct the Budget Officer to include the subject payables in budgeting considerations giving priority to the current year amounts should management find it difficult to immediately fund the whole amount of payables; and

c. Maximize the revenue-raising power of the municipality to identify additional source of revenue to fund the whole amount of payables.

Recommendations:

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to ensure strict observance of the timely release of the barangays’ share in the Municipality’s collection of RPT during the quarter by being conscious and reminding concerned personnel within their respective offices of the timelines for the processing of pertinent transactions pursuant to Section 271(d), Chapter 7, Title II, Book II of the Local Government Code.

We recommended that Management direct:
The Municipal Assessor to:
Provide assessed values to the Municipal Treasurer for billing of the RPT based on the appraisal made by and received from the Provincial Assessor's Office; and
Ensure thereafter that all properties subject to RPT be included in the physical inventory to prevent under-assessment of taxes.
b. The Municipal Treasurer to prepare and submit billing of RPT to MORESCO I and to the Provincial Assessor’s Office / Treasurer.

We recommended that Management:
Direct the GSO- Designate or the MHO to make use of the RIS in recording the receipt of drugs/medicines and supplies and subsequent issuance thereof which will be used in the preparation and submission of the SSMI at the end of each week.
Require the Accountant to:
Strictly and consistently record procurement of drugs/medicines throughout the year following the perpetual inventory method using the Drugs and Medicines Inventory account and recognize expense for the subsequent issuances based on the SSMIs submitted by the GSO/MHO;
Identify and reclassify medical supplies recognized under the Drugs and Medicines Inventory account to the proper account Medical, Dental and Laboratory Supplies Inventory.
In coordination with the Personnel Unit, review and evaluate the manning of the GSO and other units concerned, and consider detailing a staff to assist the existing personnel therein and to avoid multitasking.

We recommended that Management direct the MHO to:
Properly assess the drugs/medicines requirement of the LGU and coordinate with the DOH or other donor agencies with regards thereto; and
Inform DOH as soon as expired or soon-to-expire medicines are received from them as these may have just been overlooked by the donor government agencies.

We recommended that Management require:
The GSO to forward to the Accounting Unit all the documents pertaining to the donated land and ensure thereafter that all donated properties received by the LGU be reported to the Accountant as soon as possible for proper recognition thereof in the books; and
The Municipal Accountant to take up in the books the above donated property at its fair value at the time of acquisition and ensure thereafter that donated properties be recognized accordingly as soon as pertinent documents are received from the GSO.  Also, once initial information on property donations to LGU verbally come to his attention, coordinate, if possible, with the GSO for the prompt submission of documents relative thereto.

We recommended that management:
Instruct the concerned official/personnel to:
Hasten the preparation of documents necessary for the application for insurance of the properties; and
Revisit/refer to Section 5.1 of COA Circular No. 2018-002 for the preparation and submission of inventory reports of all insurable properties to GSIS to ensure that all insurable assets are adequately covered/insured with the GIF of the GSIS;
Ensure that a budget is provided for the insurance of all insurable properties and other assets under the custody and accountability of the Municipal government.

We recommended that Management:
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to appropriately recognize Leave Benefits Payable and Terminal Leave Benefits in the books;
Direct the Budget Officer to include the subject payables in budgeting considerations giving priority to the current year amounts should management find it difficult to immediately fund the whole amount of payables; and
Maximize the revenue-raising power of the municipality to identify additional source of revenue to fund the whole amount of payables.

We recommended that Management initiate the creation and filling in of the additional mandatory MDRRMO positions through any or a combination of the options provided under Section 5.5 of the above joint circular.

We recommended that Management require the BAC Secretariat to:
Fast track the sorting and collating of the subject contracts and supporting documents and immediately submit them to COA for evaluation and review, and thereafter ensure that copy of the purchase orders and perfected contracts be submitted to COA not later than five working days from issuance thereof;
If possible, make a formal request for the provision of full-time staff in the BAC Secretariat; and
For concerns on the staffing of the BAC Secretariat and all other concerns affecting the performance / output of the BAC, communicate such matters with concerned officials the soonest time possible for prompt action thereon.

We recommended that management require the Municipal Engineer to hasten the conduct of re-inspection of projects and immediately submit to COA the pertinent requests for inspection and supporting documents for technical evaluation and inspection, and ensure thereafter the submission of said requests and documents within reasonable time after completion and acceptance of the infrastructure projects.

We recommended that Management direct the BAC Secretariat to ensure at all times that the Performance Securities posted by winning bidders prior to the execution of the contracts are sufficient in amount and proper in form in accordance with the aforementioned provisions of the Revised IRR of RA 9184.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to ensure strict observance of the timely release of the barangays’ share in the Municipality’s collection of RPT during the quarter by being conscious and reminding concerned personnel within their respective offices of the timelines for the processing of pertinent transactions pursuant to Section 271(d), Chapter 7, Title II, Book II of the Local Government Code.

We recommended that Management require the HRMO to:
Observe diligence and strictly adhere to CSC policies, guidelines and standards on hiring and appointing personnel and other relevant matters;
Ensure that the qualification standards for newly created plantilla positions are in accordance with pertinent DBM Budget Circulars and other issuances, and keep abreast of any updates thereon posted in the DBM website; and
Per CSC recommendation, adopt innovative ways to attract the most qualified personnel to join government service.

We recommended that Management direct the GAD focal person to ensure that programs/projects/activities formulated and included in the GPB and subsequently undertaken and charged against the GAD budget are in line with the purpose for which GAD was created which, above all, should address gender issues in adherence to the requirements of PCW-NEDA-DILG JMC No. 2016-01.

We recommended that Management direct the GAD Focal Person to maximize the utilization of the GAD funds through the implementation of GAD-related programs and projects in order to attain the objective for which funds were provided.  Also, consider possible limitations in planning the activities to be undertaken so that the implementation thereof will be nonetheless feasible.

Include the leave benefits payable in budgeting considerations giving priority to

	the current year amounts should management find it difficult to immediately fund the whole amount of payables; and

observations.managementAction: The Management prioritized terminal leave benefits for CY 2021 in the amount of Php 4,392,649.20.There is no cash back up for the leave benefits and management find it difficult to fund the whole amount

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Ensure that the appropriation for EME in the Annual Budget is in compliance with the provisions prescribed in Section 325 (h) of RA No. 7160, and that the actual expenses sourced therefrom are within the limitation to avoid disallowance in audit;

Require the Municipal Budget Officer to ensure that the appropriation for EME or DF in the Annual Budget is presented under one line item only which is the EME; and

c. Require the Municipal Accountant to reclassify EME-sourced expenses recognized under other expense accounts to the Extraordinary and Miscellaneous Expenses (EME) account, and ensure thereafter that discretionary expenses, especially those sourced from the EME allocation, be classified under appropriate account/s.
observations.managementAction: Sustain the implementation

observations.reason: Deferment of GAM’s implementation (COA Circular 2021-007)

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the Accounting Unit to:

Ensure that withholding taxes are deducted from payments for honoraria in accordance with the Revised Withholding Tax Table as prescribed under Annex A of BIR Revenue Memorandum Circular No. 105-2017 dated December 28, 2017; and 

Ensure that the same are remitted to the BIR within the prescribed period.

observations.reason: Not prioritized

observations.recommendation:
It was recommended that Management observe and implement sound internal control measures in property and supply management, as provided under the NGAS Manual, to wit:

Provide property tags/ stickers to each movable property and allocate budget thereto;

Provide complete information in the Inspection and Acceptance Report and Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment, such as date of inspection and acceptance, brand names of the items, and serial numbers that would facilitate identification and tracking of the assets; and 

Prepare Requisition Issue Slips (RIS) to end-users for issued supplies.
observations.reason: No personnel
No personnel

observations.recommendation:
It was recommended to:

a. Review the submitted SCBAA and apply the necessary adjustments in accordance with the provisions of PPSAS No. 24;

b. 	Provide adequate and necessary disclosures in the Notes to Financial Statements relative to the budget information as required under PPSAS No. 24; and

c. 	Submit reviewed and finalized SCBAA and revised Notes to FS to the Audit Team.

We recommended that Management, particularly the Municipal Treasurer’s Office, to comply with Section 271 (d) of Chapter 7 under Title II – Real Property Taxation of the Local Government Code Book II and observe the timely release of the barangay’s share in its collection of real property tax during the quarter.
observations.reason: Old system in remitting was not corrected

observations.recommendation:
The following were recommended:

	a. Coordinate with the Assessor’s Office to enhance collection efficiency and take initiatives to find ways in accordance with Section 345 of RA 7160.

	b. 	Adapt strategic means or method to improve the collection efficiency through the conduct of information drive among its residents so that they will be aware of the necessity and advantage in making municipal government financially capable.
observations.managementAction: Massive tax info campaign by barangay

observations.recommendation:
a. It was recommended that the Municipal Assessor: 

1.	Complete the conduct of physical inventory of all of MORESCO’s electric posts with the assistance of the Barangay Council and to prepare the Tax Declaration thereon;
2.	Provide assessed values to the Municipal Treasurer for billing of the Real Property Taxes; and

b.	The Municipal Treasurer to prepare and submit billing of real property tax to MORESCO I and to the Provincial Assessor’s Office/ Treasurer.
observations.reason: Management still awaiting for the tax declarations of the electric posts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Planning and Development Officer to hasten the preparation of the building plan and insure the office building with the GSIS General Insurance Fund as mandated, so that in case of destruction or loss, the cost of the property would be recovered.
observations.reason: Management is yet to locate the building plans of the old buildings as it is part of GSIS’ requirement.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management, particularly the Municipal Treasurer’s Office, to comply with Section 271 (d) of Chapter 7 under Title II – Real Property Taxation of the Local Government Code Book II and observe the timely release of the barangay’s share in its collection of real property tax during the quarter.
observations.managementAction: Management will exert effort to comply with the recommendation

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management:
Require the COs to strictly adhere to the above-mentioned laws, rules and regulations on collections and deposits/remittances, seeing to it that all collections are deposited/remitted intact/complete daily or not later than the next banking day;
Provide a vault/safe for the COs’ unremitted collections and unused accountable forms in order to safeguard them from possible loss and/or misappropriation.
Management Comment:
Management resolved to ensure that the Collecting Officers remit their collections at least three times in a week and ensure that their cash on hand must not exceed their maximum cash accountability.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to immediately take up the following adjustments in the books and ensure compliance with the pertinent provisions of the GAM thereafter:
Transfer the Property, Plant and Equipment accounts from the Trust Fund to the General Fund
Trust Fund Books
General Fund Books
Adjust the carrying value and recognize the depreciation expenses for the prior years
General Fund Books
Provide for depreciation expense of the transferred PPE for the current year
General Fund Books
Management Comment:
The Office of the Municipal Accountant already recorded the necessary adjustments in the books. These adjustments were reflected and included in the February 2021 financial reports. The Management also instructed the Office of the Municipal Accountant to be acquainted with the procedures prescribed under the Government Accounting Manual (GAM) for local Government Units (LGUs).

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to:
After pertinent PPE accounts under the Trust Fund books are closed and transferred to the General Fund Books, adjust the latter using the following entries:
Prepare PPE Ledger Cards (PPELC) for the unrecognized assets and adjust accordingly the costs reflected in the PPELC of the assets which were understated;
Thereafter, use the following entries to record capital expenditures from the Trust Fund as illustrated in the GAM:
Maintain subsidiary ledgers for each infrastructure project under the Construction in Progress account.
Management Comment:
The Office of the Municipal Accountant already recorded the necessary adjusting entries in the books.  These adjustments were reflected and included in the February 2021 financial reports.  The Management also directed the Office of the Municipal Accountant to prepare PPE ledger cards for the unrecorded assets and use the journal entries prescribed under the Government Accounting Manual (GAM) for Local Government Units (LGUs).

We recommended that Management require the following:
Fast track the disposal of the obsolete unused checks in compliance with the procedures provided in the above-mentioned laws, rules and regulations; and
Ensure thereafter that all unserviceable/obsolete properties/items be properly accounted, documented and disposed accordingly.
Management Comment:
The Municipal Treasurer already accomplished the Waste Material Report for the disposal of the said obsolete unused checks. Request for inspection and approval of disposal of the obsolete checks including the waste materials report have already been submitted to the COA Office for appropriate action.

We recommended that management:
Instruct the concerned personnel to assess/determine the bond requirements of all AOs deemed appropriate based on reliable estimates such as the average monthly accountabilities of the AOs; and
Increase the amount of maximum cash accountability in the bond application of the AOs accordingly.
Management Comment:
The fidelity bond of the Disbursing Officer has been increased to P750,000 per bond risk no. 63-21-1207R. The new maximum cash accountability of the Disbursing Officer effective March 25, 2021 to March 24, 2022 is at ₱2.5M. The collectors’ fidelity bond had just been renewed with the same maximum cash accountability. Nevertheless, their bond shall be increased upon expiry and renewal thereof.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to:
Appropriately recognize Leave Benefits Payable and Terminal Leave Benefits in the books;
Include the leave benefits payable in budgeting considerations giving priority to the current year amounts should management find it difficult to immediately fund the whole amount of payables; and
Maximize the revenue-raising power of the municipality to identify additional source of revenue to fund the whole amount of leave benefits payable.
Management Comment:
The undersigned directed the Municipal Budget Office to include the leave benefits payable of the accumulated leave credits of the Municipality’s regular officials and employees in budgeting considerations starting year 2022 Budget. Moreover, the Office of the Municipal Accountant was instructed to recognize leave benefits payable once budget is available.

We recommended that Management:
Ensure that the appropriation for EME in the Annual Budget is in compliance with the provisions prescribed in Section 325 (h) of RA No. 7160, and that the actual expenses sourced therefrom are within the limitation to avoid disallowance in audit;
Require the Municipal Budget Officer to ensure that the appropriation for EME or DF in the Annual Budget is presented under one line item only which is the EME; and
Require the Municipal Accountant to reclassify EME-sourced expenses recognized under other expense accounts to the Extraordinary and Miscellaneous Expenses (EME) account, and ensure thereafter that discretionary expenses, especially those sourced from the EME allocation, be classified under appropriate account/s.
Management Comment:
The Management required the Municipal Budget Office to ensure that the appropriation for EME in the Annual Budget is in compliance with the provisions stipulated in Section 325 (h) of RA No. 7160. Moreover, the Municipal Budget Officer committed to adhere and compliance with such concern, in fact she had already addressed and corrections had already made so that 2% limitations is properly applied to the EME for CY 2021 Annual Budget. Further, the management also required the Municipal Accountant to reclassify EME-sourced expenses recognized under other expense accounts to the Extraordinary and Miscellaneous Expenses (EME) account, and ensure thereafter that Discretionary Expenses especially those sourced from the EME allocations, be classified under appropriate funds. The Management of the Local Government Unit of Gitagum would like to assure that we would strictly comply whatever recommendations given by your office and we look forward for your consideration on this matter.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to:
Adjust the Trust Fund books of the Municipality using the following entries:
After the pertinent PPE account under the Trust Fund books is closed and transferred to the General Fund Books, adjust the latter for the carrying value of the ICT equipment using the following entry:
Fast track his orientation on and being acquainted with the procedures prescribed under the GAM to ensure proper accounting of pertinent financial transactions.
Management Comment:
The Management instructed the Office of the Municipal Accountant to record the necessary adjusting entries and be acquainted with the procedures prescribed under the Government Accounting Manual (GAM) for Local Government Units (LGUs).

We recommended that Management require the Accounting Unit to:
Ensure that withholding taxes are deducted from payments for honoraria in accordance with the Revised Withholding Tax Table as prescribed under Annex A of BIR Revenue Memorandum Circular No. 105-2017 dated December 28, 2017; and
Ensure that the same are remitted to the BIR within the prescribed period.
Management Comment:
The Management already instructed the Office of the Municipal Accountant to ensure that withholding taxes are deducted from payments of honoraria of the BAC Members, TWG and BAC Secretariat and to ensure remittance of taxes withheld within the prescribed period.

observations.managementAction: Management will exert effort to comply with the recommendation
Management will exert effort to comply with the recommendation
Management will exert effort to comply with the recommendation
observations.reason: Inventory Committee was already created. There is no budget to hire for a staff to be assigned in the GSO.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management observe and implement sound internal control measures in property and supply management, as provided under the NGAS Manual, to wit:

a. 	Provide property tags/ stickers to each movable property and allocate budget thereto;

b. 	Provide complete information in the Inspection and Acceptance Report and Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment, such as date of inspection and acceptance, brand names of the items, and serial numbers that would facilitate identification and tracking of the assets; and 

c. 	Prepare Requisition Issue Slips (RIS) to end-users for issued supplies.
observations.managementAction: Management will exert effort to comply with the recommendation
observations.reason: Labeling could not be done due to no physical inventory conducted

observations.managementAction: Management will exert effort to comply with the recommendation

observations.recommendation:
a. 	 We have required the Municipal Accountant to: 

1. 	Reclassify to Construction in Progress accounts for the cost of projects not yet completed;
 
2. 	For future similar transactions, use the Construction Period Theory by recognizing Construction in Progress accounts and transferring the cost of completed assets to their respective asset accounts.

3. 	Provide disclosures in the Notes to FS for projects partially completed under Construction in Progress account, as well as construction cost not yet billed by the contractor. The Construction in Progress account should approximately reflect the actual percentage of completion as to the cost of construction projects.

b. 	Require the Office of the Municipal Engineer to regularly prepare and submit reports of completed projects to the Accounting Unit.
observations.managementAction: Management will exert effort to comply with the recommendation
Management will exert effort to comply with the recommendation.

b. 	Based on the report given, Municipal Accounting Unit to recognize accounts receivable and receipt from market operations.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended the following:

a. 	Require the Municipal Account to revisit COA Circular 2015-009 for the proper classification of accounts in recording financial transactions of the Municipality pursuant to the Revised Chart of Accounts for LGUs; 

b. 	Ensure that the expenditures for meals and snacks are necessary and responsive to the exigency of service; and

c. 	Employ the efficient and economic utilization of the funds.

b. 	Provide adequate and necessary disclosures in the Notes to Financial Statements relative to the budget information as required under PPSAS No. 24; and

c. 	Submit reviewed and finalized SCBAA and revised Notes to FS to the Audit Team.

observations.managementAction: Management will exert effort to comply with the recommendation

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended the following:

a. 	Coordinate with the Assessor’s Office to enhance collection efficiency and take initiatives to find ways in accordance with Section 345 of RA 7160.

b. 	Adapt strategic means or method to improve the collection efficiency through the conduct of information drive among its residents so that they will be aware of the necessity and advantage in making municipal government financially capable.
observations.managementAction: Management will exert effort to comply with the recommendation

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended the following:

a. 	The Municipal Assessor to:

1. 	Complete the conduct of physical inventory of all of MORESCO’s electric posts with the assistance of the Barangay Council and to prepare the Tax Declaration thereon;

2. 	Provide assessed values to the Municipal Treasurer for billing of the Real Property Taxes; and

b. 	The Municipal Treasurer to prepare and submit billing of real property tax to MORESCO I and to the Provincial Assessor’s Office/ Treasurer.
observations.reason: Request has already been submitted to the Provinvila Assessor’s Office. Still waiting for the result and approval thereof.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management prepare an Operations Manual that establishes procedures in leasing out its market stalls, clearly defining responsibilities of each office involved in the preparation and termination of lease contracts, billing and collection of monthly rental income, to enhance transparency in its market operations, ensure that controls are in place and adequate documentation and records are maintained.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer to transfer the DSWD funds to the Trust Fund depository accounts and the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary correcting entries. Moreover, we also require the Municipal Accountant to inform and provide the correct bank account to the DSWD Region 10.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Planning and Development Officer to hasten the preparation of the building plan and insure the office building with the GSIS General Insurance Fund as mandated, so that in case of destruction or loss, the cost of the property would be recovered.

We recommended that management make representation with the National Water Resource Board to facilitate compliance with the Code specifically on the acquisition of Water Permit for all the water sources locations within the municipality and appeal for reconsideration of penalties that the Board may imposed.  Accordingly, prepare the necessary required documentary requirements for application for Water Permit in all water sources developed, managed and administered by the municipality and its barangays.

We recommended that Management observe and implement sound internal control measures in property and supply management, as provided under the NGAS Manual, to wit:
Provide property tags/ stickers to each movable property and allocate budget thereto;
Provide complete information in the Inspection and Acceptance Report and Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment, such as date of inspection and acceptance, brand names of the items, and serial numbers that would facilitate identification and tracking of the assets; and
Prepare Requisition Issue Slips (RIS) to end-users for issued supplies.

We recommended that Management,
Require the Municipal Accountant to:
Reclassify to Construction in Progress accounts for the cost of projects not yet completed;
For future similar transactions, use the Construction Period Theory by recognizing Construction in Progress accounts and transferring the cost of completed assets to their respective asset accounts.
Provide disclosures in the Notes to FS for projects partially completed under Construction in Progress account, as well as construction cost not yet billed by the contractor. The Construction in Progress account should approximately reflect the actual percentage of completion as to the cost of construction projects.
Require the Office of the Municipal Engineer to regularly prepare and submit reports of completed projects to the Accounting Unit.

We recommended that Management require the following:
Municipal Treasurer’s Office to prepare report of unpaid/uncollected rent income based on the subsidiary ledgers at the end of each reporting date; and
Based on the report given, Municipal Accounting Unit to recognize accounts receivable and receipt from market operations.

We recommended that Management:
Require the Municipal Account to revisit COA Circular 2015-009 for the proper classification of accounts in recording financial transactions of the Municipality pursuant to the Revised Chart of Accounts for LGUs;
Ensure that the expenditures for meals and snacks are necessary and responsive to the exigency of service; and
Employ the efficient and economic utilization of the funds.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant to:
Review the submitted SCBAA and apply the necessary adjustments in accordance with the provisions of PPSAS No. 24;
Provide adequate and necessary disclosures in the Notes to Financial Statements relative to the budget information as required under PPSAS No. 24; and
Submit reviewed and finalized SCBAA and revised Notes to FS to the Audit Team.

We recommended that Management, particularly the Municipal Treasurer’s Office, to comply with Section 271 (d) of Chapter 7 under Title II – Real Property Taxation of the Local Government Code Book II and observe the timely release of the barangay’s share in its collection of real property tax during the quarter.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
Coordinate with the Assessor’s Office to enhance collection efficiency and take initiatives to find ways in accordance with Section 345 of RA 7160.
Adapt strategic means or method to improve the collection efficiency through the conduct of information drive among its residents so that they will be aware of the necessity and advantage in making municipal government financially capable.

We recommended that Management direct:
The Municipal Assessor to:
Complete the conduct of physical inventory of all of MORESCO’s electric posts with the assistance of the Barangay Council and to prepare the Tax Declaration thereon;
Provide assessed values to the Municipal Treasurer for billing of the Real Property Taxes; and
The Municipal Treasurer to prepare and submit billing of real property tax to MORESCO I and to the Provincial Assessor’s Office/ Treasurer.

We recommended that Management,
Require the Municipal Accountant to:
Reclassify to Construction in Progress accounts for the cost of projects not yet completed;
For future similar transactions, use the Construction Period Theory by recognizing Construction in Progress accounts and transferring the cost of completed assets to their respective asset accounts.
Provide disclosures in the Notes to FS for projects partially completed under Construction in Progress account, as well as construction cost not yet billed by the contractor. The Construction in Progress account should approximately reflect the actual percentage of completion as to the cost of construction projects.
Require the Office of the Municipal Engineer to regularly prepare and submit reports of completed projects to the Accounting Unit.

We recommended that Management require the following:
Municipal Treasurer’s Office to prepare report of unpaid/uncollected rent income based on the subsidiary ledgers at the end of each reporting date; and
Based on the report given, Municipal Accounting Unit to recognize accounts receivable and receipt from market operations.

We recommended that Management:
Require the Municipal Account to revisit COA Circular 2015-009 for the proper classification of accounts in recording financial transactions of the Municipality pursuant to the Revised Chart of Accounts for LGUs;
Ensure that the expenditures for meals and snacks are necessary and responsive to the exigency of service; and
Employ the efficient and economic utilization of the funds.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant to:
Review the submitted SCBAA and apply the necessary adjustments in accordance with the provisions of PPSAS No. 24;
Provide adequate and necessary disclosures in the Notes to Financial Statements relative to the budget information as required under PPSAS No. 24; and
Submit reviewed and finalized SCBAA and revised Notes to FS to the Audit Team.

We recommended that Management, particularly the Municipal Treasurer’s Office, to comply with Section 271 (d) of Chapter 7 under Title II – Real Property Taxation of the Local Government Code Book II and observe the timely release of the barangay’s share in its collection of real property tax during the quarter.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
Coordinate with the Assessor’s Office to enhance collection efficiency and take initiatives to find ways in accordance with Section 345 of RA 7160.
Adapt strategic means or method to improve the collection efficiency through the conduct of information drive among its residents so that they will be aware of the necessity and advantage in making municipal government financially capable.

We recommended that Management direct:
The Municipal Assessor to:
Complete the conduct of physical inventory of all of MORESCO’s electric posts with the assistance of the Barangay Council and to prepare the Tax Declaration thereon;
Provide assessed values to the Municipal Treasurer for billing of the Real Property Taxes; and
The Municipal Treasurer to prepare and submit billing of real property tax to MORESCO I and to the Provincial Assessor’s Office/ Treasurer.

a.	Require the Municipal Treasurer and the Disbursing Officer to return unclaimed cash benefits to the trust fund and attach the Official Receipt in the liquidation;

b.	Municipal Accountant, to stamp “PAID” each payroll sheet to prevent reprocessing of these documents for payments and to clearly identify the supporting payrolls for each check disbursement by indicating the check number on each payroll sheet.

observations.reason: Lack Personnel

observations.recommendation:
a. 	Municipal Treasurer’s Office to prepare report of unpaid/uncollected rent income based on the subsidiary ledgers at the end of each reporting date; and

b.	Based on the report given, Municipal Accounting Unit to recognize accounts receivable and receipt from market operations.

We recommended that Management prepare an Operations Manual that establishes procedures in leasing out its market stalls, clearly defining responsibilities of each office involved in the preparation and termination of lease contracts, billing and collection of monthly rental income, to enhance transparency in its market operations, ensure that controls are in place and adequate documentation and records are maintained.

We recommended that Management revise or amend the Penalty Clause for past due accounts under Paragraph 6 of the Lease Contract to comply with the limit set by Section 168 of RA 7160 and Municipal Revenue Code of 2011.

We recommended that Management require its Treasurer’s Office to apply the correct water rates in billing the Commercial/Institutional/Industrial Consumers in accordance with the Municipal Revenue Code of 2011.
We recommended that Management prepare and enter into valid water service contract with its clients.

We recommended that Management require the GAD Focal Person to submit annually the Gender and Development Plan and Budget as well as the Accomplishment Report and encourage the management through the GAD Focal Person to sustain or improve the program by formulating and implementing more GAD related programs to establish gender awareness among the officials and employees and the clientele.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer to transfer the DSWD funds to the Trust Fund depository accounts and the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary correcting entries. Moreover, we also require the Municipal Accountant to inform and provide the correct bank account to the DSWD Region 10.
observations.reason: The Accountant and Treasurer committed to implement in CY 2020. They failed to implement in CY 2019 because they could not determine the final balance to be transferred to the TF due to ongoing disbursements and downloading.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to close the dormant accounts and return the cash balance to the appropriate funds and bank accounts.

In order to comply with the provisions of RA 9003, we recommended that Management allocate funds for this purpose and:

a. 	Develop the Municipal’s Solid Waste Management Plan and submit the same to the National Solid Waste Management Board for review and approval

b.	Municipal Solid Waste Management Board to closely monitor the compliance and implementation of the provisions under RA 9003
We recommended that Management monitor the implementation of these projects so that the purpose for which the funds were granted is achieved and the projects would benefit its constituents.
observations.reason: Of the six projects, only one was not implemented which is the tilapia production. The Municipal Accountant was waiting for the go signal from the DA to return unutilized funds.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Planning and Development Officer to hasten the preparation of the building plan and insure the office building with the GSIS General Insurance Fund as mandated, so that in case of destruction or loss, the cost of the property would be recovered.
observations.reason: The Management has difficulty in securing the documents required by GSIS such as building plans. However, the Management already allotted budget for the insurance premiums.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Bids and Award Committee Secretariat to furnish the Auditor within five (5) working days from their execution, copies of contracts, and purchase orders, together with their supporting documents to enable the Auditor to make a timely review and recommend actions necessary to make correction or adjustment at the earliest time possible.

We recommended that management make representation with the National Water Resource Board to facilitate compliance with the Code specifically on the acquisition of Water Permit for all the water sources locations within the municipality and appeal for reconsideration of penalties that the Board may imposed.  Accordingly, prepare the necessary required documentary requirements for application for Water Permit in all water sources developed, managed and administered by the municipality and its barangays.
observations.reason: Still on process

observations.recommendation: Municipal Accountant-Designate to strictly adhere to the prescribed time frame for the submission of monthly accounts and financial reports to the Office of the Auditor not later than the 20th day after the end of each month as prescribed under Section 70 of the NGAS Manual.  Likewise, turn over the receipts and disbursement records with all paid vouchers and documents evidencing the transaction to the Auditor within 10 days of the ensuing month in compliance of Section 7.2.1(a) of COA Circular No. 2009-006.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:
Require the Accountant, the General Services Officer and the Inventory Committee to follow through the procedures provided in COA Circular 2015-008 for the physical count, reconciliation, recognition and measurement of infrastructure assets recorded in the registry of public infrastructures;
Ensure that the required records and reports are prepared and maintained by both the Accountant and the GSO; and
Submit to the COA Auditor the required reports and supporting schedules for review and validation.

We recommended that Management require the following:
Municipal Treasurer’s Office to prepare report of unpaid/uncollected rent income based on the subsidiary ledgers at the end of each reporting date; and
Based on the report given, Municipal Accounting Unit to recognize accounts receivable and receipt from market operations.

We recommended that Management prepare an Operations Manual that establishes procedures in leasing out its market stalls, clearly defining responsibilities of each office involved in the preparation and termination of lease contracts, billing and collection of monthly rental income, to enhance transparency in its market operations, ensure that controls are in place and adequate documentation and records are maintained.

We recommended that Management revise or amend the Penalty Clause for past due accounts under Paragraph 6 of the Lease Contract to comply with the limit set by Section 168 of RA 7160 and Municipal Revenue Code of 2011.

We recommended that Management require its Treasurer’s Office to apply the correct water rates in billing the Commercial/Institutional/Industrial Consumers in accordance with the Municipal Revenue Code of 2011.
We recommended that Management prepare and enter into valid water service contract with its clients.

We recommended that Management require the GAD Focal Person to submit annually the Gender and Development Plan and Budget as well as the Accomplishment Report and encourage the management through the GAD Focal Person to sustain or improve the program by formulating and implementing more GAD related programs to establish gender awareness among the officials and employees and the clientele.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer to deposit all her collections intact daily or not later that the next banking day to abide by the regulation and to avoid the risk of possible loss of government money

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer to transfer the DSWD funds to the Trust Fund depository accounts and the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary correcting entries. Moreover, we also require the Municipal Accountant to inform and provide the correct bank account to the DSWD Region 10.
observations.reason: The Accountant and Treasurer committed to implement in CY 2019. They failed to implement in CY 2018 because they could not determine the final balance to be transferred to the TF due to ongoing disbursements and downloading.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the following:

a. Municipal Accountant to use the proper accounts in accordance to COA Circular 2015-009 and the accounting entries as illustrated in Annex D of COA Circular 2012-002, 

b. Municipal Accountant to revert the CY 2011 and 2012 unexpended balance in the General Fund

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to:

a. Conduct a regular review, reconciliation and monitoring of all booked receivables to ensure  that these are collected when these become due and demandable and that cash advances and fund transfers are liquidated within the prescribed period depending on their nature and purpose. 

b. Analyze the accounts that are dormant and long outstanding and recognized impairment for accounts no longer collectible.

c. Exert diligent efforts to locate the supporting documents necessary for the preparation of request for write-off pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005.

observations.reason: Lack Personnel

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to close the dormant accounts and return the cash balance to the appropriate funds and bank accounts.

In order to comply with the provisions of RA 9003, we recommended that Management allocate funds for this purpose and:

a. Develop the Municipal’s Solid Waste Management Plan and submit the same to the National Solid Waste Management Board for review and approval

b. Municipal Solid Waste Management Board to closely monitor the compliance and implementation of the provisions under RA 9003
We recommended that Management monitor the implementation of these projects so that the purpose for which the funds were granted is achieved and the projects would benefit its constituents.

We recommended that Management require the Local Development Council to:
a. Review all their plans and targets to ensure that what is planned and targeted are achievable and/or implementable during the calendar year;

b. Optimize the utilization of the 20% Development Fund through proper planning of the projects including timing of execution, coordinated action among LGU Officials for better results and strict monitoring in project implementation to ensure the completion of the project; and

c. Maximize the use of the fund for the implementation of the projects geared towards social, economic and environmental development of the locality that positively affect the lives of its constituents.

We recommended that Management require the following actions:

a. GFPS to make representations to the Sangguniang Bayan to expedite the approval of the Municipal Ordinance to enact the Gender and Development Code of the Municipality of Gitagum; and

b. GFPS to prepare the GAD Accomplishment Report in accordance to Annex E of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Circular No. 2016-01 and be as detailed as possible in the activities conducted and nature of expense charged against the GAD Budget to facilitate audit review and verification.

c. GFPS to closely monitor and keep records to track its actual expenditures charged against the GAD Budget and ensure that GAD activities conducted conformed to the activities as prioritize in the GAD Plan and Budget.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Planning and Development Officer to hasten the preparation of the building plan and insure the office building with the GSIS General Insurance Fund as mandated, so that in case of destruction or loss, the cost of the property would be recovered.

We recommended that Management require the Bids and Award Committee Secretariat to furnish the Auditor within five (5) working days from their execution, copies of contracts, and purchase orders, together with their supporting documents to enable the Auditor to make a timely review and recommend actions necessary to make correction or adjustment at the earliest time possible.

We recommended that management make representation with the National Water Resource Board to facilitate compliance with the Code specifically on the acquisition of Water Permit for all the water sources locations within the municipality and appeal for reconsideration of penalties that the Board may imposed.  Accordingly, prepare the necessary required documentary requirements for application for Water Permit in all water sources developed, managed and administered by the municipality and its barangays.
