observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the following:
LGU Administrator to secure the Memorandum of Agreement for the project;
Municipal Treasurer to issue Official Receipt for the cash overage counted and to deposit under Trust Fund as Other Payables; and
Municipal Treasurer and Accountant to communicate and facilitate return of funds to the Source Agency.

Recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer to deposit all her collections intact daily or not later that the next banking day to abide by the regulation and to avoid the risk of possible loss of government money.

Recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the Municipal Treasurer to transfer the DSWD funds to the Trust Fund depository accounts and the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary correcting entries. Moreover, we also require the Municipal Accountant to inform and provide the correct bank account to the DSWD Region 10.

Recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the following:
Municipal Accountant to use the proper accounts in accordance to COA Circular 2015-009 and the accounting entries as illustrated in Annex D of COA Circular 2012-002,
Municipal Accountant to revert the CY 2011 and 2012 unexpended balance in the General Fund; and
Management Comment:
The Municipal Accountant resolved to use the appropriate accounts starting in CY 2018. Moreover, she stated that the unexpended CY 2011 and 2012 LDRRM Funds will be reverted when there are projects planned to be implemented using the unused funds.

Recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to:
Conduct a regular review, reconciliation and monitoring of all booked receivables to ensure  that these are collected when these become due and demandable and that cash advances and fund transfers are liquidated within the prescribed period depending on their nature and purpose.
Analyze the accounts that are dormant and long outstanding and recognized impairment for accounts no longer collectible.
Exert diligent efforts to locate the supporting documents necessary for the preparation of request for write-off pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005.

Recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to close the dormant accounts and return the cash balance to the appropriate funds and bank accounts.

Recommendation:
In order to comply with the provisions of RA 9003, we recommended that Management allocate funds for this purpose and:
Develop the Municipal’s Solid Waste Management Plan and submit the same to the National Solid Waste Management Board for review and approval
Municipal Solid Waste Management Board to closely monitor the compliance and implementation of the provisions under RA 9003

Recommendation:
We recommended that Management monitor the implementation of these projects so that the purpose for which the funds were granted is achieved and the projects would benefit its constituents.

Recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the following actions:
GFPS to make representations to the Sangguniang Bayan to expedite the approval of the Municipal Ordinance to enact the Gender and Development Code of the Municipality of Gitagum; and
GFPS to prepare the GAD Accomplishment Report in accordance to Annex E of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Circular No. 2016-01 and be as detailed as possible in the activities conducted and nature of expense charged against the GAD Budget to facilitate audit review and verification.
GFPS to closely monitor and keep records to track its actual expenditures charged against the GAD Budget and ensure that GAD activities conducted conformed to the activities as prioritize in the GAD Plan and Budget.

Recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the following actions:
Management to ensure that funds to be appropriated for GAD is only for Gender and Development PPAs and that there is separate appropriations for the MCPC, the Senior Citizen and the PWD;
GFPS to prepare the GAD and Plan Budget in accordance to the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Circular No. 2016-01; and
GFPS to closely monitor the implementation of its projects, programs and activities to effectively identify and resolve issues and problems on Gender and Development that should be given utmost attention in the locality.

observations.reason: Physical count was conducted and the RPCPPE was submitted to the Auditor but was not yet reconciled with the Accounting Records

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant-Designate to submit to COA a Report on Utilization of Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund for timely review and in adherence to existing law.

We recommended that Management require the Bids and Award Committee Secretariat to furnish the Auditor within five (5) working days from their execution, copies of contracts, and purchase orders, together with their supporting documents to enable the Auditor to make a timely review and recommend actions necessary to make correction or adjustment at the earliest time possible.

We recommended that management make representation with the National Water Resource Board to facilitate compliance with the Code specifically on the acquisition of Water Permit for all the water sources locations within the municipality and appeal for reconsideration of penalties that the Board may imposed.  Accordingly, prepare the necessary required documentary requirements for application for Water Permit in all water sources developed, managed and administered by the municipality and its barangays.
observations.reason: Still on process

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to require the following:

Instruct the Local Finance Committee that at least 5% of the annual appropriation be allocated for GAD related activities as provided under RA 7192, EO 243, and DBM-NEDA-PCW Joint Circular No. 2012-01. 

Require the LGU GAD Focal Point (GFP) to strictly comply and adhere the provisions on the preparation, submission, review and endorsement of Agency GAD Plans and Budget as illustrated under Annexes A, B, & C of the attached PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Circular No. 20013-01.

We recommended that management:

Require the Municipal Mayor to coordinate with the Assessor’s Office to enhance collection efficiency and take initiatives to find ways. Use the assessment roll in setting up the targeted receivables-Real Property Tax to arrive at a more realistic and reliable amount, thereby avoiding a material amount of receivable which could be doubtful as to collectability. 

Adapt strategic means or method to improve the collection efficiency through the conduct of information drive among its residents so that they will be aware of the necessity and advantage in making municipal government financially capable.

Require the Municipal Accountant to: 
Reclassify cost of projects not yet completed to Construction in Progress accounts.

For future similar transactions, use the Construction Period Theory by recognizing Construction in Progress accounts and transferring the costs of completed assets to their respective asset accounts.

Provide disclosures in the Notes to FS for projects partially completed but not billed by the contractor. The Construction in Progress account should have approximately reflected the actual percentage of completion as to cost for the construction projects.

Require the Office of the Municipal Engineer to regularly prepare and submit reports for the completed projects to the Accounting Unit.
observations.managementAction: Municipal Accountant was instructed to use the Construction in Progress accounts in the ensuing years and the Municipal Engineer to regularly submit status reports of the projects to the Accounting Unit.

Municipal Accountant was instructed to reclassify the completed projects to the proper asset accounts and the Municipal Engineer to regularly submit status reports of the projects to the Accounting Unit

observations.recommendation: Management to strictly comply with the provision of DBM Budget Circular No. 2004-5A dated October 7, 2005 and Budget Circular No. 2007-3 dated November 29, 2007.
observations.managementAction: Management will comply with the recommendation

observations.recommendation: Municipal Accountant-Designate to strictly adhere to the prescribed time frame for the submission of monthly accounts and financial reports to the Office of the Auditor not later than the 20th day after the end of each month as prescribed under Section 70 of the NGAS Manual.  Likewise, turn over the receipts and disbursement records with all paid vouchers and documents evidencing the transaction to the Auditor within 10 days of the ensuing month in compliance of Section 7.2.1(a) of COA Circular No. 2009-006.
observations.managementAction: Management will comply with the recommendation

observations.reason: Given insufficient attention due to several designations. Management committed to conduct inventory count in CY 2017

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Agency should instruct the Inventory Committee to submit the IIRUP.  Require the Accounting Office to reclassify the obsolete, non-functional and those no longer used in the operation (as shown in the IIRUP) from their respective asset accounts to Other Assets account.  Create an Appraisal Committee to evaluate the unserviceable properties, comply with the documents required under Annex M.E. of COA Memorandum 2005-027, and recommend the applicable mode of disposal of the items in a Disposal Committee resolution for the Local Chief Executive’s approval.  Submit these documents to the Auditor for evaluation and action thereon prior to the disposal of unserviceable items.
observations.reason: Management committed to conduct first inventory count of all properties in CY 2017 to determine which assets are still functional and for disposal

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Planning and Development Officer to hasten the preparation of the building plan and insure the office building with the GSIS General Insurance Fund as mandated, so that in case of destruction or loss, the cost of the property would be recovered.
observations.reason: Already submitted application for insurance at GSIS. Awaiting inspection of GSIS personnel

observations.recommendation:
We recommended to require the following:

Instruct the Local Finance Committee that at least 5% of the annual appropriation be allocated for GAD related activities as provided under RA 7192, EO 243, and DBM-NEDA-PCW Joint Circular No. 2012-01. 

Require the LGU GAD Focal Point (GFP) to strictly comply and adhere the provisions on the preparation, submission, review and endorsement of Agency GAD Plans and Budget as illustrated under Annexes A, B, & C of the attached PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Circular No. 20013-01.
observations.reason: GAD Plans and Accomplishment Report were not yet submitted to COA. GAD Plan for CY 2017 had been endorsement to DILG and for submission to COA

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation
We recommended that Management:
Require the Municipal Accountant to:
Reclassify cost of projects not yet completed to Construction in Progress accounts.
For future similar transactions, use the Construction Period Theory by recognizing Construction in Progress accounts and transferring the costs of completed assets to their respective asset accounts.
Provide disclosures in the Notes to FS for projects partially completed but not billed by the contractor. The Construction in Progress account should have approximately reflected the actual percentage of completion as to cost for the construction projects.
Require the Office of the Municipal Engineer to regularly prepare and submit reports for the completed projects to the Accounting Unit.

Recommendation:
We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Planning and Development Officer to hasten the preparation of the building plan and insure the office building with the GSIS General Insurance Fund as mandated, so that in case of destruction or loss, the cost of the property would be recovered.

Recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the Bids and Award Committee Secretariat to furnish the Auditor within five (5) working days from their execution, copies of contracts, and purchase orders, together with their supporting documents to enable the Auditor to make a timely review and recommend actions necessary to make correction or adjustment at the earliest time possible.

Recommendation:
We recommended that Management strictly comply with the provision of DBM Budget Circular No. 2004-5A dated October 7, 2005 and Budget Circular No. 2007-3 dated November 29, 2007.

Recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant-Designate to strictly adhere to the prescribed time frame for the submission of monthly accounts and financial reports to the Office of the Auditor not later than the 20th day after the end of each month as prescribed under Section 70 of the NGAS Manual.  Likewise, turn over the receipts and disbursement records with all paid vouchers and documents evidencing the transaction to the Auditor within 10 days of the ensuing month in compliance of Section 7.2.1(a) of COA Circular No. 2009-006.

Recommendation:
We recommended that management make representation with the National Water Resource Board to facilitate compliance with the Code specifically on the acquisition of Water Permit for all the water sources locations within the municipality and appeal for reconsideration of penalties that the Board may imposed.  Accordingly, prepare the necessary required documentary requirements for application for Water Permit in all water sources developed, managed and administered by the municipality and its barangays.

Recommendation:
We recommended that Management require the following:
Instruct the Local Finance Committee that at least 5% of the annual appropriation be allocated for GAD related activities as provided under RA 7192, EO 243, and DBM-NEDA-PCW Joint Circular No. 2012-01.
Require the LGU GAD Focal Point (GFP) to strictly comply and adhere the provisions on the preparation, submission, review and endorsement of Agency GAD Plans and Budget as illustrated under Annexes A, B, & C of the attached PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Circular No. 20013-01.

Recommendation:
We recommended that management:
Direct the Municipal Treasurer to enhance collection efficiency
Instruct the Municipal Accountant to use the assessment roll in setting up the targeted receivables-Real Property Tax (RPT) to arrive at a more realistic and reliable amount, thereby avoiding a material amount of receivable which could be doubtful as to collectability.
Adapt strategic means or method to improve the collection efficiency through the conduct of information drive among its residents so that they will be aware of the necessity of paying the RPT

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant – Designate to conduct analysis/review of the accounts, if it is not possible due to the absence of records and documents, request for write-off and/or adjustments of account balances from the Commission on Audit.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant-Designate to submit to COA a Report on Utilization of Local Disaster Risk Reduction Management Fund for timely review and in adherence to existing law.

We recommended that Management and its Local School Board should strictly observed Section 272 of the Local Government Code of 1991 and DECS-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 01 series of 1998 on SEF utilization. It should stop providing appropriation for MOOE expenses in the Local School Board Budget which are not in accordance with the above cited law/circular, instead charge all ineligible budget items under Section 272 to the regular DepEd Budget.

We recommended that Management require the Bids and Award Committee Secretariat to furnish the Auditor within five (5) working days from their execution, copies of contracts, and purchase orders, together with their supporting documents to enable the Auditor to make a timely review and recommend actions necessary to make correction or adjustment at the earliest time possible.
observations.reason: There were still delays in the submission of contracts but the number of days of delay had decrease to 32 days or less

observations.recommendation: We recommended to require the Management to strictly comply the Memorandum of Agreement.
We recommended to require the Management to secure copy of the Memorandum of Agreement (MOA).

We recommended that management make representation with the National Water Resource Board to facilitate compliance with the Code specifically on the acquisition of Water Permit for all the water sources locations within the municipality and appeal for reconsideration of penalties that the Board may imposed.  Accordingly, prepare the necessary required documentary requirements for application for Water Permit in all water sources developed, managed and administered by the municipality and its barangays.
observations.reason: Already applied for the Water Permit from NWRB. Awaiting the grant of permit

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Management should direct the Municipal Treasurer to remit the amount withheld to the BIR pursuant to RA 8291, PD 1752 and RR No. 2-98 so that the same could be utilized by the recipient agency for the benefit of their constituents/members and to avoid penalties due to delayed remittance.

We recommended that management:

Require the Municipal Mayor to coordinate with the Assessor’s Office to enhance collection efficiency and take initiatives to find ways. Use the assessment roll in setting up the targeted receivables-Real Property Tax to arrive at a more realistic and reliable amount, thereby avoiding a material amount of receivable which could be doubtful as to collectability. 

Adapt strategic means or method to improve the collection efficiency through the conduct of information drive among its residents so that they will be aware of the necessity and advantage in making municipal government financially capable.
observations.reason: Accountant committed to adjust receivable account to reflect realistic and fair value of the receivable as recommended by the Auditor
